$comment: c27 (D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE): the NEGATIVE MISCOMPILE pin for a volatile POINTEE (red-on-disable: exit 3 with the flag, exit 2 without). main() reads `*p` twice (p is `volatile int *`) across an opaque poke() CALL padded above the inline threshold. CSE's load-admission scans only Stores -> a Call is not a clobber -> a NON-volatile pointee would have its 2nd `*p` FOLDED to the 1st (b==a==1 -> exit 2, the miscompile). The MirInstFlags::Volatile thread on each deref (derefResultType yields VolatileQual(int) -> volatileFlagForType sets the flag) makes CSE skip the fold -> b re-fetches 2 -> exit 3. The release shippedPipeline arm runs the real optimizer (Inlining/CSE/Mem2Reg), so this is an end-to-end witness. The pointee-specific companion to volatile_member (scalar global). No stdout (libc-free); exit code only. arm64-elf runs under qemu.
